observations.recommendation:
Update the recording of all reconciling items of the book balance so that the outstanding reconciling items can be properly taken up in the books; and 
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer on the balances of accounts already closed for reconciliation and adjustment.
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Unit prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the account to Trust Liability Account – Conequip Philippines Inc.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile its records with the Municipal Accountant’s Office at least quarterly in order to account any discrepancies immediately.

The designated General Services Officer to cause the immediate renewal of all the LGU's delinquent and invalid motor vehicle registrations in compliance with RA 4136 otherwise known as the Land Transportation and Traffic Code; and include the renewal of the insurance coverage/policies of the same motor vehicles with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and COA Circular 92-390; and 
The designated General Services Officer to continue exerting more effort to facilitate the registration and transfer of the motor vehicles in the name of the Municipality in accordance with Section 7 of RA 4136 and to locate the missing documents to expedite the registration and transfer of the same.

Instruct the Property Unit to issue demand letters to those accountable officers for those missing PPEs established during the physical count, and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others;
General Services Officer (GSO)-Designate and Municipal Accountant (MA) to maintain proper and complete Property Cards (PCs) and PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs), respectively, for easy determination, identification, and reconciliation of records.

Further, the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs be strictly implemented in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, in order to properly dispose and drop the missing properties from the books of accounts

We recommended management to submit perfected contracts and POs including its complete supporting documents on time to facilitate timely review and determination of deficiencies for immediate correction/adjustment
The BAC, Municipal Engineer, MPDC and MDC properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion.

Instruct the drivers concerned to serially number the trip tickets and   properly fill up all pertinent information thereof: and
Appoint/designate an employee to strictly monitor the utilization of vehicles and to submit the required Monthly Report on Official Travels and Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption to evaluate the proper use of all transportations and fuel consumption to prevent possible wastage of funds.

Fill in the vacant positions to avoid the dilemma of hiring JOs and/or COS appointees and to augment and help carry out the functions of the municipality which are supposedly reserved to permanent employees whose appointment are based on the rigid scrutiny of merit and fitness selection criteria developed by the CSC, thus ensuring a high-quality government service. Also, maximize the services of available personnel so as not incur additional expenses; and
Evaluate the monthly rates based on the skills and job description of the JOs/Cos.

The Municipal Treasurer send written notices to the payees of the existence of the stale checks at least one month before a check becomes stale; the Municipal Accountant make an adjusting entry by debiting Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account and crediting Accounts Payable Account to restore the liability to creditor; and the Municipal Treasurer issue new checks to replace the stale checks upon its presentation;
The Municipal Accountant prepare monthly BRS and submit the same within the reglementary period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 together with its supporting documents such as Monthly Bank Statement or Snapshot to facilitate the early submission of the BRS and Subsidiary Ledgers to facilitate verification and analysis of cash account.

Require the periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. Physical count of unissued inventory items by type which shall be conducted at least once a year as required in GAM and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

Conduct a thorough analysis, in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer, to determine which among the projects included as part of the CIP accounts were completed or not;
Maintain index for each type of CIP account containing relevant information such as but not limited to (name of project, location, funding source, total project cost, duration of the project, total cost incurred to date & progress billing) for monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all the entries of the general ledger and subsidiary ledger of the Other Payable account to determine the causes of abnormal balance and make appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant send demand letters to those with outstanding accounts receivables including the amount granted as livelihood programs. If accounts may be written off, the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 be observed.
The Municipal Accountant locate the supporting documents for projects implemented by the LGU at Barangay Gatub and Barangay Dagum and send demand letters; and
The Municipal Accountant identify the NGAs and NGOs/POs, send confirmation letters and reconcile any discrepancies.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to proceed with the prescribed procedures in recognition of PPEs found at station and disposition for non-existing/missing PPEs in order to reconcile the results of the ‘one-time cleansing’ physical count with the accounting records which shall then be considered as the correct balance of the agency’s PPE. Thereafter, properly update the records and books of any subsequent acquisition or disposal of PPEs to arrive at the correct balance as of year-end.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct:
The MAO and GSO to jointly undertake the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records to establish accurate and reliable balances;
The GSO to review the RPCPPE and ensure the exclusion of semi-expendable items, which are properly classified under the appropriate inventory accounts, in accordance with the chart of accounts and relevant COA guidelines; and
Expedite the disposal of unserviceable or obsolete properties as identified in the IIRUP, in accordance with the 2024 Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties (COA-DBM [Department of Budget and Management] Joint Circular No. 2024-1), to prevent overstatement of the PPE balance.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to the following:
The GSO to immediately coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office to validate and assess the donated parcel of land and facilitate the titling of all untitled properties;
The GSO retrieve and consolidate all relevant supporting documents such as Deeds of Absolute Sale, Deeds of Donation, and Tax Declarations for the identified parcels of land. These documents be submitted to the MAO for the proper recording and recognition of the said assets in the accounting books; and
The non-existent parcel of land valued at P40,000.00 be thoroughly investigated, and appropriate adjustments be made in the accounting records, if warranted, to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the PPE balances in the FS.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO prepare and maintain complete PPELCs and PCs, respectively, and conduct periodic reconciliation of their records and update the same based on the results of the physical inventory.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the MEO to closely coordinate and reconcile their respective records to accurately identify all completed projects. The MEO to submit the Certificate of Completion and other supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant who shall ensure the timely reclassification of these completed projects from the CIP account to the appropriate asset accounts, in accordance with applicable accounting standards and guidelines.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year, based on the RPT Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card, which shall serve as the basis for booking the RPT and SET Receivable accounts;
The Municipal Accountant analyze and identify the source of the P23.190 million variance between the recorded RPT and SET receivables and the certified master list of delinquencies, and to prepare the necessary adjusting JEV to recognize the unrecorded receivables; and
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per the master list of RPT delinquencies with the RPT and SET Receivable account balances in the books of accounts to ensure a more accurate presentation of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Comply with the proper disclosure of the MF in NFS as required in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and classify the same as MOOE and CO based on the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan; and
Adjust the recorded unexpended balance of MF-MOOE by recording the identified error in computation to reflect the correct balance of TL-DRRMF account.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to trace prior year’s records and accounting entries which affect TL-DRRMF account to identify the actual utilization and facilitate the recording of necessary adjustments of TL-DRRMF account, if warranted. Otherwise, revert back to the GF any unutilized amount of LDRRMF previously transferred to the TL – DRRMF account after the allowable five years which shall be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Accurately identify the status of all CIP accounts under DRRM Fund and secure necessary documents for all completed PPAs in coordination with the MEO to facilitate the recording of these items for adjustment, if warranted, to reflect the correct balance of TL-DRRM account for the fair presentation of the account in the FS; and
Identify the discrepancy and provide appropriate adjusting entries for items 5 and 6 to correct the previous year’s erroneous entries.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to examine the discrepancy between the reported Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account and the corresponding Cash in Bank – LCCA and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant to conduct analysis of the Accounts Payable account and, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the subject accounts payable into its appropriate liability accounts; and
The Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze transactions/payables, properly record the payables pertaining to the receipt of goods or services on account in the normal course of trade and business operation as accounts payable, and record other payables into their appropriate liability accounts, taking into consideration the description of accounts in the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to correctly reclassify the interest expense and ensure that financial transactions are reviewed and recorded using the appropriate accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 (Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs).

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA with SFPER, in compliance with Section 47 of IPSAS No. 24, to ensure the accuracy and reliability of its financial reporting; and
Moving forward, the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that interest expenses are properly charged against Financial Expenses appropriations, whether under continuing appropriations or other fund sources.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding comparative figures in the current year's FS, in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 54 of IPSAS 3. Furthermore, the required disclosures be included in the Notes to the FS.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer identify the discrepancies between the ledger and cashbook balances and make the necessary adjustments to reconcile them; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic reconciliation of balances between the accounting ledgers and the Treasury’s cashbooks at least quarterly.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to ensure that no cash advances for the PCF shall be granted to elected officials. Cash advances granted to elective official is limited for travelling expenses and the Confidential Fund for the Local Chief Executive, as the LGU’s Head of Agency.
We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documentary requirements in the granting and replenishment of PCF.
We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the use of Imprest System in the maintenance of PCF.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct PCCs to fully liquidate the cash advances for PCF at year-end by preparing the Report of Disbursements and its supporting documents and return the unused cash, if any, to the Municipal Treasurer who shall issue an Official Receipt which will be attached to the liquidation.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to ensure that immediate steps were made to facilitate and expedite the registration and transfer of titles for the purchased and donated parcels of land. This is essential to establish legal ownership and fully safeguard the interests of the LGU.

We recommended the following:
The LDRRM Council to ensure that only disaster risk reduction and management activities are included in its LDRRMF Investment Plan and the annual budget for the 5 per cent LDRRMF. Funding for capital outlay of the LDRRM Office be included in the appropriations under the DRRM Office; and
Refrain from charging against LDRRMF expenditures which are not directly related to the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management activities namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Treasurer:
Maintain and update regularly the Check Register and reconcile the same with the RCI and bank statements to ensure completeness and reliability of financial information;
Include only the released/issued checks to the payees in the monthly RCI for submission to the Accounting Office; and
The Municipal Accountant to record the transactions based on individual checks duly received and acknowledged by the payee that are supported with complete documentation.

The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in the reconciliation and presentation of amounts under the local and external sources of revenue in the SCBAA; and
The Municipal Budget Officer prepare the Registry of Appropriations and Obligations for continuing funds of the 20% EDF, present correctly the amount under the capital outlay and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to correctly reflect the amounts in the SCBAA.

The Municipal Accountant ensure that balances reported in the LDRRM Funds and disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements are accurate and include other sources of funds (segregating the donations received, bank interest on deposits and IRA difference if any); and
The Municipal Budget Officer maintain three (3) separate RAO for QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay. Any balances of the Capital Outlay be maintained as continuing appropriations under the General Fund, unless re-programmed to MOOE and can be transferred to the STF.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the misstatements of the asset accounts mentioned above.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the balance of the reverted DRRM funds from Trust Liability-DRRMF to income account and record its proper asset or expense account for any disbursements thereof. Lastly for the MDRRM Council request to the local sanggunian to issue a resolution for a Supplemental Budget for the reverted unexpended funds of the LDRRM in the General Fund and re-program the amount for other social services PPAs.
We recommended that the management adhere to Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the submission of cash advance voucher for CF together with the documentary requirements.

We recommended that management exercise due diligence in ensuring that goods procured through public bidding and delivered are covered with performance and warranty security in compliance with RA 9184.

We recommended that management direct the PESO Manager to submit the distribution lists showing proof of receipt by each beneficiary and direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly require distribution lists in all types of payments that involved distribution of goods and materials to the intended beneficiaries.

Submit a written explanation and detailed justification on the deficiencies noted in the Technical Inspection Report; and
Require the supplier to provide a warranty security of the equipment.

We recommended that the management prepare the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same on a monthly basis on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of the COA Circular No.2012-002 to monitor disbursements.

The Municipal Mayor prioritize the creation of mandatory    positions     of the three staff responsible for administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning, in accordance with Civil Service Commission (CSC) rules and regulations and subject to the limitations imposed under Local Government Code; and

The MDRRM Officer:
Prepare and establish plans, policies, and systems that are not yet in place:
Accreditation of volunteers and institutionalizing Community Response Team.
Designation of Public Information Officer of its alternate on its communication system.
Frequency of conduct of drills both to its clients and employees.
Recovery Plan
Business Continuity Plan
Service Delivery System
Storage/Warehouse Management System
Update outdated policies and plans to ensure proper accountability and responsibility:
Contingency Plans on earthquake and flashflood.
Forced evacuation ordinance.
Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS)
Municipal Internal communication protocols
Evacuation maps
Institutionalized early warning and alarm system
Consider the procurement of basic equipment, tools, gears, and expendable items that do not exist in the current inventory as one of the PPAs in the MDRRM Plan and allocate funds thereof.

We recommended that management submit a written explanation on the necessity of the above-listed disbursements. If found unwarranted, a corresponding disallowance will be issued.
Likewise, management discontinue the incurrence of excessive and unnecessary catering services for meals and snacks and  expenses for food and snacks for meetings, session/gatherings must be charged to the monthly representation allowance of the department heads which is the very purpose of their allowance.
Set-up and/or record only accounts payable with valid and complete supporting documents in adherence to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445

Payments for Transportation Allowance to be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing by prorating and deducting from the Transportation Allowance of officials who are actually using the government service vehicle on official travels outside their official stations.

Ensure that procurement of services is awarded only to eligible contractors/suppliers per Section 53.1, revised IRR, RA No. 9184 in cases of Two Failed Biddings and has complied with the requirements as stated in the Notice of Award.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other in order to prevent future delays in computations and preparations of pertinent accounting documents to facilitate the prompt release of any unreleased shares to the barangays concerned relative to their 50% share of community tax or cedula and 25% share on RPT collections, as mandated.

Maintain equipment logbooks showing the time and date of release and return of the heavy equipment; 
Require duly approved and pre-numbered Equipment Rental Release Order (ERRO) for every release of heavy equipment.

It is recommended that the LCE refrain from granting of additional cash advances to the DO unless the previous CAs are fully liquidated or settled.  Please be advised to strictly observe the regulation on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

It is recommended that the LCE refrain from granting cash advances without legally specific purpose, otherwise he will be held responsible for improper granting, utilization and liquidation of the same.

It is recommended that management make representation to COA ICFAU and follow up on the issuance of credit notices for the liquidation of confidential fund and strictly observe the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and Items 4 and 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

It is recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the Municipal Inventory Committee will conduct the annual physical count of the inventory and to prepare and submit the RPCI to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

It is recommended that the OIC - Municipal Accountant maintain complete and accurate supplies ledger cards, PPE ledger cards, work, other animals and breeding stock ledger card and the GSO shall maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies, property, plant and equipment and work animals.

It is recommended that after the conduct of the physical count by the Inventory Committee, the committee, OIC – Municipal Accountant and GSO reconcile the RPCPPE as against the General Ledgers, ledger cards and property cards and the Inventory Committee to completely fill up the columns required in the RPCPPE.
It is recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate an employee to act as authorized inspector of goods and services.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the error made.

It is recommended that in the succeeding fiscal years, management include sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for SC and PWD and for Implementation of the programs of the LCPC as required under RA 9344, RA 9442 amending RA 7277 and DILG MC 2014-59.
It is recommended that the LGU open a separate bank account for SFP and SPP as required in the MOA and Section 2.4 of the Omnibus Guidelines.
It is recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer accepts Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment for progress billings and procurement of services.

It is recommended that management submit to this Office the perfected contract and issued POs including its supporting documents within five working days from contract execution or PO issuance together with complete supporting documents.
It is recommended that the management issue the Notice to Proceed within two calendar days from the date of approval of the contract.
It is recommended that Pag-Ibig Contribution for employer share be reduced to P100.00 per employee in compliance with Section 7 of RA No. 9679.
It is recommended that AOs be adequately bonded in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

It is recommended that the unexpended LDRRMF for CYs 2015 and 2016 be transferred to the Special Trust Fund and the concerned personnel shall ensure that the total unexpended balance of QRF and LDRRMF - MOOE will be transferred to the Special Trust Fund at the end of the year.

It is recommended that the LSB refrain from providing for non-priority expenditures in the SEF budget. Instead, it should allocate SEF funds for the construction and repair of school buildings; facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition of books and periodicals; and sports development. Also, the LSB should evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth in the RA 7160 and 5447.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall make proper adjustments to reclassify the account to “Due from National Government Agencies” to reflect the proper transaction.

It is recommended that management ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% EDF are implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PPAs that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive efforts should be made to fast track the completion of the PPAs and ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs identified in AIP.
It is recommended that the GAD FPS monitor the implementation of GAD activities and ensure that the GAD fund will be fully utilized to attain it goals and objectives.
It is recommended that the GAD FPS and the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the GAD budget is being spent for what it was intended for.
It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant shall reconcile their records at least quarterly pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated Feb. 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconciled their records.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management shall adhere to Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the documentary requirements prior to granting and release of cash advances for Confidential Fund to the accountable officer and ensure that Peace and Order Program (POP) is appropriated in the Annual Budget and must be incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan as a priority concern of the municipality to support the allocation for CF.  Otherwise, absence of such, allocation of CF is invalid and unnecessary.
observations.managementAction: There was no grant of cash advances for CF for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that transactions and events are recognized and recorded only when they occur supported with proper and complete documentation, ensure compliance with the principles and mandates of the PPSAS in recognizing and recording assets, liabilities and expense accounts, work back the transactions recorded in the Accounts Payable with the corresponding debits in the appropriate asset and expense accounts to determine the transactions that has actually occurred and completed and adjust accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall use appropriate accounts in recording wages charged to ongoing projects and make adjustments to entry made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the adjustments to the entry made.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall analyze transactions to ensure proper recording and classifying of accounts; and make necessary adjustments in the books to properly reflect the correct classification of transactions to appropriate accounts as suggested in the above table.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation

observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall analyze transactions to ensure proper recording and classifying of accounts; and make necessary adjustments in the books to properly reflect the correct classification of transactions to appropriate accounts as suggested in the above table.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant recorded the adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the MSWDO shall properly monitor the implementation and liquidation of the Day Care Workers of the funds for supplemental feeding not later than 30 days after the end of a month’s feeding so as not to delay the subsequent releases of funds from the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the MSWDO properly monitor the implementation and liquidation of the social pension program to ensure timely delivery of the benefits to the recipients.
observations.managementAction: MSWDO complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Management shall ensure that in cases of fund transfer to NGOs/POs and to another agency, it must be supported with complete documentation pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and the Municipal Accountant shall properly record the transfer of fund to NGOs/POs and to other agencies as receivable for proper accounting and to determine accountability of the transferred funds.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Management shall ensure that utilization of 20% Development Fund be for capital expenditure and/or in the nature of investments and refrain from appropriating PAPs which are not qualified to be funded out of the 20%EDF.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer shall ensure that allocation for 20% EDF shall be properly computed and appropriated based on its actual receipts of IRA.
observations.managementAction: MBO complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the MDC and the MPDC as the MDC Secretatriat shall prepare an approved Supplemental AIP for newly identified and qualified PAPs that will be sourced out of the 20% EDF to be submitted to Sangguniang Bayan for adoption and ensure that any additional 20% EDF shall be programmed for qualified PPAs and supported by approved supplemental AIP.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Management shall ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% EDF shall be implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PPAs that are still unimplemented and ongoing, positive efforts should be made to fast track the completion of the PPAs and ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs identified in AIP.
observations.reason: Some PPAs of the previous year are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall open a separate bank account for the 20% EDF only and shall transfer the remaining balance of the EDF as of December 31, 2015 and the Municipal Accountant is directed to maintain a separate SL for the 20% EDF to ensure that receipt and charges out from this fund will be closely monitored.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer opened a separate bank account for the EDF with DBP last April 11, 2016 and a separate SL has been maintained by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the MDRRMO shall prepare in addition to the Work and Financial Plan, an annual LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the MDRRMO shall prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same to the COA as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall analyze transactions to ensure proper recording and classifying of accounts and make necessary adjustments in the books to properly reflect the correct classification of transactions to appropriate accounts as suggested in the above table.

Also, the Municipal Treasurer shall only receive from the collectors the actual cash remitted base on the Report of Collections and Deposits.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management shall ensure that all disbursements of government funds must be supported with complete documentation. The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that prior to processing of disbursement vouchers; the supporting documents must be complete, valid, and compliant with the existing laws, rules and regulations for disbursements. On the utilization of fuel, submission of Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Report of Official Travels with the Approved Drivers’ Ticket be made to COA for verification. Refrain from using Reimbursement Expense Receipts (RER) for expenditures which can be done or made from a duly licensed establishment who can issue valid official receipts. Hiring of consultants must be in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the HRMO properly evaluate the documents supporting the claim for monetization especially when it exceeds 30 days and the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that documents are complete to support the payment for monetization claims in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management ensure that in hiring job orders and/or contracts of service personnel, it shall be limited to works for local projects to prevent irregular disbursements of government funds. 
The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the affected accounts and record the wages of the JOs/COS personnel to proper accounts to reflect the correct amount of expenditures for each account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the LGU shall pay only obligations with the contracting party.  Refrain from paying the Supplier prior to the delivery and acceptance of the goods procured.  Ensure strict compliance with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR.  Demand payment from the Supplier the amount representing the total cost of opening the letter of credit.
observations.managementAction: Payment was made by the supplier to the LGU for the cost of the letter of credit.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management shall contact the supplier and discuss the results of inspection conducted by the RTITS. Require the supplier to pay for the difference of the prices due to non-conformance to the PO of the engine model and capacity of the heavy equipment delivered. Place a mark-“Entered duty and tax free under the Local Government Code-RA 7160” on the heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: The supplier justified that, as industry practice, there is a margin for variance on power rating in heavy equipment output and only a small difference in the power rating resulting to less than 10% slippage.  In fact, the bulldozer deliver has a greater output that what was technically specified in the PO.
observations.reason: The justification made by the supplier will be forwarded to RTITS for their comments.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer shall ensure that allocation/appropriation for Miscellaneous Expenses shall be within the prescribed limitation for discretionary fund which is 2% of the actual receipts of the basic real property tax of the next preceding calendar year pursuant to Section 325(h) of the RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: Management shall cause the transfer of PPE recorded in the Trust Fund amounting to P12,125,186.01 to the recipient barangays by way of deed of donation or transfer without cost and shall instruct the barangay bookkeeper to record these PPE in their respective books in accordance with the provisions stipulated in a MOA or other legal documents supporting the implementation of these projects.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Refrain from appropriating PAPs that are not in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No 2011-1 and include only those that are related or connected to the implementation of development projects; and

Prohibit from utilizing the 20% EDF for expenditures not related to and/or connected to the implementation of the 20% EDF for expenditures not related to and/or connected to the implementation of development projects, activities and programs.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer shall maintain a separate special account for the 20% EDF as provided in Section 313 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: In the succeeding years, the Local budget Officer shall include in the Annual Budget appropriation for SC and PWD and Two Percent for Implementation of the programs of the LCPC as required under RA No. 9442 amending RA Nos. 7277 and 9344 so that PPAs for them can be properly executed.
observations.reason: Insufficient appropriation were provided for SC and PWD and for implementation of the LCPC for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to restitute the missing funds and deposit the amounts to the authorized depository bank to avoid facing charges of technical malversation for failure to render accounts as provided for in Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.
	
Institute appropriate administrative and legal sanction to the concerned Accountable Officer to ensure recovery of the missing funds.
observations.reason:
The previous treasurer was already dismissed from office.

The LGU is in the process of looking for means to recover the shortage from the previous Mun. Treasurer.

The previous Municipal Treasurer died in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Finance Committee shall carefully determine the income from local sources that are reasonably realizable and attainable.

Regular monitoring of collections against targets and the need to revisit Tax Ordinances   and steep up collection efficiency.

The local finance committee should recommend measures to increase their income.
                                                                                                             
Refrain from bloating estimated income to avoid incurrence of budget deficits.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, still has a short fall of revenue by P589,397.02.

observations.recommendation: The management should review and revise their job order contracts and start paying wages on a daily and no work no pay basis to conform to the guidelines provided in the CSC Res. No. 02-0790 dated June 5, 2002 re: “The Policy Guidelines for Contract of Services.
observations.reason: There were some contractual workers who were paid on a monthly basis due to the necessity of their service even beyond office hours.

observations.recommendation:
The management appropriate 20% EDF exclusively to Programs/Projects and activities which are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with the above cited Joint DBM-DILG Circular.  Programs, projects and activities should be specifically identified, quantified and accomplished in accordance with plans and targets set for the year. 

The Municipal Development Council should be apprised of the existing regulations and guidelines set forth in DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2010-138 and 2011-001, in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% EDF of the Internal Revenue Allotment to include only the most meaningful and rooted projects that would contribute to the development of the LGU and would benefit the constituents.

The Local Finance Committee shall monitor that funds intended for the projects and programs for the current and continuing appropriations are  readily available otherwise these could not be implemented.
observations.reason: Almost all programs and projects were in accordance with the guidelines in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% EDF.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator, together with the Municipal Development Council, to carefully evaluate every proposed project indicated in the Project Profile of the proponent offices and ensure to include only the most meaningful and rooted developmental projects and programs for   consolidation in the Annual Investment Plan.
	
The provisions provided for in DILG – Budget Joint Memorandum Circular No.1, s. 2005 dated September 20, 2005 and reiterated in DILG Cir. No. 2010-138 should be adhered to.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendations.
